rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes, using only visible feature names and constants.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers or property references not present in the AADL model.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or helper declarations within the same annex for AGREE expressions.
Ensure type consistency between helper declarations and their usage in AGREE contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks within component type or implementation scopes.
Do not reference AADL timing properties (e.g., Deadline, Period) as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed.
anti_patterns: Do not assume that AADL property names like Compute_Execution_Time are automatically available in AGREE scope.
Do not reference identifiers or types that are not visible or declared in the same annex.
irrelevant_topics: Implementation-level bindings and subcomponent port usage in component type contracts
AGREE validator diagnostics and repair strategies
Model fusion and owner/scope preservation rules
